projectDescription: SQLa is a web application for learning SQL through visual block-based programming, allowing students to construct and execute SQL queries by dragging and connecting blocks.
keyFeatures: Visual block-based SQL editor powered by Google Blockly
Support for DDL statements (CREATE TABLE with columns, constraints, primary keys, and foreign keys)
Support for DML statements (SELECT with JOIN, WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING, ORDER BY, DISTINCT, TOP)
Aggregate functions (SUM, AVG, COUNT, MIN, MAX) with GROUP BY and HAVING support
Isolated session-based schema per user for safe query execution against SQL Server
Real-time ERD diagram generation from CREATE TABLE blocks using Mermaid
Live SQL query execution against Microsoft SQL Server backend
SQL export to .sql file and workspace export to JSON
Security layer blocking dangerous SQL commands (DROP DATABASE, EXEC, GRANT, etc.)
publicApiSurface: POST /api/sql/session — Create a new isolated session with its own SQL Server schema
GET /api/sql/session/:id — Validate and retrieve an existing session
POST /api/sql/analyze — Parse, validate, transform, and execute a SQL statement
GET /api/sql/history/:sessionId — Retrieve query history for a session
GET /api/sql/export-sql/:sessionId — Export all session queries as a .sql file
